spec_match.rationale: Uses `::column { scroll-snap-align: start }` on a multi-column container — correct usage — but combines `overflow-x: auto` with `scroll-snap-type: x mandatory` and `columns: 3 220px`. Multi-column fragments stack along the block axis by default, so horizontal-x snap needs `column-fill: auto` plus a height constraint to actually produce a horizontal row of columns.

interactive.rationale: Real horizontal-scroll gallery with prev/next buttons that scrollBy(220px, smooth). Six paragraph cards driven by multi-column engine.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises paragraph stream paginated by multicol with each generated column a snap target — exactly what's wired.

edge_cases.rationale: Doesn't show behaviour at narrow viewports (`columns: 3 220px` shorthand falls back to fewer columns); the unsupported-browser fallback claim `cards snap on width` doesn't hold without the pseudo.

design_system.rationale: All colours via CSS variables; mobile-responsive; no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Verify the scroll-snap-type axis matches column flow
openQuestions.detail: Multi-column fragments stack along the block axis (vertical by default). Setting `scroll-snap-type: x mandatory` on a vertical-flow container needs `column-fill: auto` plus a height constraint to produce a horizontal row. Verify on Chrome 135 that the demo actually renders cards horizontally — there's a real risk it stacks vertically.

openQuestions.title: Explain the columns shorthand semantics
openQuestions.detail: `columns: 3 220px` is `column-count: 3, column-width: 220px` — browser picks the actually-rendered count based on container width. Note this so authors understand why count varies.

openQuestions.title: Pair with v135 scroll-marker pseudo
openQuestions.detail: Lede hints at this but doesn't wire it. Add paginated dots underneath using `::scroll-marker` from the same milestone — that's the headline CSS-only carousel.

openQuestions.title: Improve unsupported-browser fallback
openQuestions.detail: Claims `cards snap on width` as fallback. Without `::column`, no snap on width unless explicit scroll-snap-align is added per card. Either implement properly or remove the misleading text.

summary: Right intent, but horizontal-scroll + vertical-flow + scroll-snap-x is fragile and needs verification. Biggest win: pair with v135 scroll-marker pseudo to land the CSS-only-carousel claim.
